# MSBuild Item Management Patterns

Canonical patterns for working with item groups, from `Microsoft.Common.CurrentVersion.targets`.

## Include / Remove / Update — Three Operations

| Operation | Purpose | When to use |
|---|---|---|
| `Include` | Add new items to the group | Creating items with identity + metadata |
| `Remove` | Remove items matching a pattern | Excluding files or clearing a group |
| `Update` | Modify metadata on existing items | Adding/changing metadata without re-adding |

### Include — Add Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="Generated\*.cs">
    <AutoGen>true</AutoGen>
  </Compile>
</ItemGroup>
```

### Remove — Subtract Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <!-- Remove specific items -->
  <Reference Remove="$(AdditionalExplicitAssemblyReferences)" />

  <!-- Set subtraction: prior minus current -->
  <_CleanOrphanFileWrites Include="@(_CleanPriorFileWrites)"
      Exclude="@(_CleanCurrentFileWrites)" />

  <!-- Clear an entire group -->
  <_Temporary Remove="@(_Temporary)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

### Update — Modify Existing Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <EmbeddedResource Update="@(EmbeddedResource)"
      Condition="'%(NuGetPackageId)' == 'Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Collections'">
    <GenerateSource>true</GenerateSource>
    <ClassName>Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Collections.SR</ClassName>
  </EmbeddedResource>
</ItemGroup>
```

`Update` does not add items — it only modifies items already in the group.

## Item Batching — %(Metadata)

When `%(Metadata)` appears in target attributes or task parameters, MSBuild **batches** execution per unique metadata value.

### Target-level batching (Outputs)

```xml
<Target Name="GenerateSatelliteAssemblies"
    Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects);@(_SatelliteAssemblyResourceInputs)"
    Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)%(Culture)\$(TargetName).resources.dll">
  <!-- Runs once per unique Culture value -->
</Target>
```

### Task-level batching

```xml
<Copy SourceFiles="@(_SourceItems)"
    DestinationFiles="@(_SourceItems->'$(OutDir)%(TargetPath)')">
</Copy>
```

### Per-item filtering with Condition

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <_ResxOutput Include="@(EmbeddedResource->'%(OutputResource)')"
      Condition="'%(EmbeddedResource.WithCulture)' == 'false'" />
</ItemGroup>
```

### Batching rules

- `%(Metadata)` in `Condition` or `Outputs` → target batches per unique value.
- `%(Metadata)` in task parameters → task batches per unique value.
- **Do not mix `%()` from different item groups** in the same expression — this causes a cross-product (see Common Pitfalls).

## Item Transforms — @(Item->'expression')

Transforms create new item lists by applying an expression to each item:

```xml
<!-- Transform file paths to destinations -->
<Copy SourceFiles="@(IntermediateAssembly)"
    DestinationFiles="@(IntermediateAssembly->'$(OutDir)%(Filename)%(Extension)')"/>

<!-- Transform with separator for display -->
<Message Text="Files: @(Compile->'%(Filename)', ', ')" />
```

## Exclude Pattern — Set Subtraction on Include

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="**\*.cs" Exclude="Generated\**;Tests\**" />
</ItemGroup>
```

`Exclude` only works on `Include` — it cannot be used with `Update` or `Remove`.

## Conditional Item Inclusion

```xml
<!-- Condition on ItemGroup — all or nothing -->
<ItemGroup Condition="'$(NetCoreBuild)' == 'true'">
  <PackageReference Include="System.IO.Pipelines" />
</ItemGroup>

<!-- Condition on individual items -->
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="System.IO.Pipelines"
      Condition="'$(NetCoreBuild)' == 'true'" />
</ItemGroup>
```

## PrivateAssets on Tool/Analyzer Packages

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers" PrivateAssets="all" />
  <PackageReference Include="StyleCop.Analyzers" PrivateAssets="all" />
</ItemGroup>
```

## Common Pitfalls

### Cross-product batching

Referencing `%(Metadata)` from two different item groups creates O(N×M) executions:

```xml
<!-- BAD: Cross-product of @(Source) × @(Config) -->
<Exec Command="process %(Source.Identity) with %(Config.Identity)" />

<!-- GOOD: Reference one group via batching, the other via property -->
<Exec Command="process %(Source.Identity) with $(ConfigFile)" />
```

### Generated files in source tree

Write to `$(IntermediateOutputPath)` (obj/), not the source directory. Source-tree generation pollutes version control and can cause duplicate compilation via globs.

### Missing FileWrites

Every file created during a target must be added to `@(FileWrites)` for `dotnet clean` support.

# MSBuild Item Management Patterns

Canonical patterns for working with item groups, from `Microsoft.Common.CurrentVersion.targets`.

## Include / Remove / Update — Three Operations

| Operation | Purpose | When to use |
|---|---|---|
| `Include` | Add new items to the group | Creating items with identity + metadata |
| `Remove` | Remove items matching a pattern | Excluding files or clearing a group |
| `Update` | Modify metadata on existing items | Adding/changing metadata without re-adding |

### Include — Add Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="Generated\*.cs">
    <AutoGen>true</AutoGen>
  </Compile>
</ItemGroup>
```

### Remove — Subtract Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <!-- Remove specific items -->
  <Reference Remove="$(AdditionalExplicitAssemblyReferences)" />

  <!-- Set subtraction: prior minus current -->
  <_CleanOrphanFileWrites Include="@(_CleanPriorFileWrites)"
      Exclude="@(_CleanCurrentFileWrites)" />

  <!-- Clear an entire group -->
  <_Temporary Remove="@(_Temporary)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

### Update — Modify Existing Items

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <EmbeddedResource Update="@(EmbeddedResource)"
      Condition="'%(NuGetPackageId)' == 'Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Collections'">
    <GenerateSource>true</GenerateSource>
    <ClassName>Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Collections.SR</ClassName>
  </EmbeddedResource>
</ItemGroup>
```

`Update` does not add items — it only modifies items already in the group.

## Item Batching — %(Metadata)

When `%(Metadata)` appears in target attributes or task parameters, MSBuild **batches** execution per unique metadata value.

### Target-level batching (Outputs)

```xml
<Target Name="GenerateSatelliteAssemblies"
    Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects);@(_SatelliteAssemblyResourceInputs)"
    Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)%(Culture)\$(TargetName).resources.dll">
  <!-- Runs once per unique Culture value -->
</Target>
```

### Task-level batching

```xml
<Copy SourceFiles="@(_SourceItems)"
    DestinationFiles="@(_SourceItems->'$(OutDir)%(TargetPath)')">
</Copy>
```

### Per-item filtering with Condition

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <_ResxOutput Include="@(EmbeddedResource->'%(OutputResource)')"
      Condition="'%(EmbeddedResource.WithCulture)' == 'false'" />
</ItemGroup>
```

### Batching rules

- `%(Metadata)` in `Condition` or `Outputs` → target batches per unique value.
- `%(Metadata)` in task parameters → task batches per unique value.
- **Do not mix `%()` from different item groups** in the same expression — this causes a cross-product (see Common Pitfalls).

## Item Transforms — @(Item->'expression')

Transforms create new item lists by applying an expression to each item:

```xml
<!-- Transform file paths to destinations -->
<Copy SourceFiles="@(IntermediateAssembly)"
    DestinationFiles="@(IntermediateAssembly->'$(OutDir)%(Filename)%(Extension)')"/>

<!-- Transform with separator for display -->
<Message Text="Files: @(Compile->'%(Filename)', ', ')" />
```

## Exclude Pattern — Set Subtraction on Include

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="**\*.cs" Exclude="Generated\**;Tests\**" />
</ItemGroup>
```

`Exclude` only works on `Include` — it cannot be used with `Update` or `Remove`.

## Conditional Item Inclusion

```xml
<!-- Condition on ItemGroup — all or nothing -->
<ItemGroup Condition="'$(NetCoreBuild)' == 'true'">
  <PackageReference Include="System.IO.Pipelines" />
</ItemGroup>

<!-- Condition on individual items -->
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="System.IO.Pipelines"
      Condition="'$(NetCoreBuild)' == 'true'" />
</ItemGroup>
```

## PrivateAssets on Tool/Analyzer Packages

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.NetAnalyzers" PrivateAssets="all" />
  <PackageReference Include="StyleCop.Analyzers" PrivateAssets="all" />
</ItemGroup>
```

## Common Pitfalls

### Cross-product batching

Referencing `%(Metadata)` from two different item groups creates O(N×M) executions:

```xml
<!-- BAD: Cross-product of @(Source) × @(Config) -->
<Exec Command="process %(Source.Identity) with %(Config.Identity)" />

<!-- GOOD: Reference one group via batching, the other via property -->
<Exec Command="process %(Source.Identity) with $(ConfigFile)" />
```

### Generated files in source tree

Write to `$(IntermediateOutputPath)` (obj/), not the source directory. Source-tree generation pollutes version control and can cause duplicate compilation via globs.

### Missing FileWrites

Every file created during a target must be added to `@(FileWrites)` for `dotnet clean` support.

data.reasoningText:
Let me analyze the issues:

1. **Cross-product batching** (line 53): `%(ProtoFile.Filename)` and `%(ValidationRule.Filename)` reference two different item groups in the same task parameter, causing 3×2=6 executions instead of the expected behavior.

2. **CS2002 duplicate source files** (line 16-19): The SDK auto-globs `**/*.cs` including `*.g.cs`. Then line 16 explicitly includes `**\*.g.cs` again → duplicates. Also, line 36 adds generated files from `obj/` which the SDK glob may also pick up.


3. **FileWrites in static ItemGroup** (line 42-43): The issue is that `FileWrites` is declared statically in the project file before the target runs, but the `Clean` target tries to read it to know which files to remove. Since the files haven't been generated yet at evaluation time, `FileWrites` is empty when `Clean` executes, so nothing gets cleaned up properly.

4. **CleanGeneratedCode target** (line 58-62): This target removes items from the `Compile` group but doesn't actually delete the physical files from disk. The glob pattern `**\*.g.cs` is also too broad and could match unintended files. The real problem is that removing from `Compile` doesn't clean up the generated files themselves.

Looking at the root causes more carefully: the SDK's default `*.cs` globbing includes generated files, creating duplicates. The cleanup logic tries to remove these from compilation but doesn't delete the actual files, and the `FileWrites` tracking doesn't work because it's evaluated before generation happens.

For the generated file inclusion issue, the files are correctly placed in the intermediate output directory, but the target adds them to `Compile` which causes them to be included twice — once from the SDK glob and once from the explicit include statement. The SDK's default excludes filter out `obj/` and `bin/`, so generated files there aren't auto-globbed, but line 16's `**\*.g.cs` pattern lacks those exclusions and will match files in `obj/` anyway. This creates two duplicate inclusion problems: source-level `.g.cs` files get picked up by both the SDK glob and line 16, while generated files in `obj/` are included by both line 16 and line 36. Now I'm looking at the FileWrites placement issue. The `FileWrites` item should be captured by the standard `IncrementalClean` target, so the static ItemGroup declaration should work fine. But looking at the `CleanGeneratedCode` target, it's using a very broad `**\*.g.cs` glob pattern that would indiscriminately remove any generated files matching that pattern, not just the ones this transform actually created. The FileWrites mechanism should only track the three generated files, so clean should delete just those. But the `**\*.g.cs` glob in CleanGeneratedCode might be too aggressive — it could remove hand-written `.g.cs` files or ones from other generators. I'm also wondering if FileWrites in a static ItemGroup is sufficient across incremental builds, or if the GenerateProtoCode target needs to run again to properly track what was generated. not including generated files in the Compile item group when the target is skipped due to incrementality. The fix involves changing line 16 from `Include` to `Update` so it only adds metadata to SDK-globbed items rather than creating duplicates, then moving the generated file compilation into a static ItemGroup that always runs outside the target. I also need to address the cross-product batching issue in the validation logic,  ensure FileWrites tracking happens inside the target where files are created, and make CleanGeneratedCode more targeted to avoid over-cleaning.

data.reasoningText:
Can't use `%(Metadata)` in Condition on a static ItemGroup like that. Let me fix this - I need to use a different approach. I can either use a target that always runs to add the generated files, or just include them without a condition (they'll be missing on first build but the target will create them).

Actually, the simplest approach: just include them unconditionally. If they don't exist yet, MSBuild will just have empty items since the transform won't match any files. Wait, no — the `Include` with a transform `@(ProtoFile->...)` will always produce items regardless of whether the files exist.


I'll create a target that runs before CoreCompile and conditionally adds the generated files to the Compile group if they exist.

data.content:
Incremental build correctly skips `GenerateProtoCode` but still compiles the generated files via `IncludeGeneratedProtoCode`. Here's a summary of the 5 fixes:

| Issue | Root Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| **Validation runs 6× (3×2)** | Cross-product batching: `%(ProtoFile.Filename)` + `%(ValidationRule.Filename)` in same expression | Batch on `%(ProtoFile)` only; reference rules via `@(ValidationRule->'...')` transform |
| **CS2002 duplicate warnings** | `Include="**\*.g.cs"` re-adds files already globbed by SDK | Changed to `Update` (modifies metadata without re-adding) |
| **Clean too broad** | `Remove="**\*.g.cs"` removes all `.g.cs` files | Scoped to `@(ProtoFile->'...%(Filename).g.cs')` transform |
| **Files not tracked across builds** | `Compile Include` was inside incremental target — skipped when up-to-date | Moved to separate `IncludeGeneratedProtoCode` target that always runs |
| **FileWrites misplaced** | Static ItemGroup outside any target | Moved inside `GenerateProtoCode` target for proper clean tracking |
